core_takeaway: Agent_1 beat Agent_3 because the whole round converged around Agent_1's opening frame: keep Apple and Pencil together for Agent_1, give Agent_2 a good Quill+Stone bundle, and leave Agent_3 with Jewel plus a promise of possible later improvement. Agent_2 had enough payoff to reinforce that frame, while Agent_3's stronger preferred bundle was deferred rather than negotiated into the current allocation.
why_or_counterexample: Yes. The first position looks advantaged in this transcript because Agent_1 used the first public turn to define the main skeletons and then rejected the one that would help Agent_3. Once Agent_2 endorsed the Apple+Pencil baseline and Agent_3 accepted it for speed and future optional refinements, all formal proposals became the same allocation: Agent_1 received Apple+Pencil for 84 utility, while Agent_3 received only Jewel for 36.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Agent_1 opened by naming Apple and Pencil as its top priorities and proposed a skeleton where it kept both. Agent_2 quickly supported that baseline because it also received Quill+Stone. Agent_3 initially argued for Skeleton B, which would give it Jewel+Quill+Stone, but later accepted Skeleton A to keep the negotiation moving and rely on a possible Round 2 upgrade.
phase_notes.private_thinking: Private thinking is present for all three agents. The private notes repeatedly describe Skeleton A as the viable unanimous baseline because it preserves Agent_1's top items; even Agent_3's private plan shifts from its higher-value Skeleton B toward locking Skeleton A now and negotiating Skeleton C later.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: All three formal proposals allocate Apple+Pencil to Agent_1, Quill+Stone to Agent_2, and Jewel to Agent_3, with only item order differences for Agent_2. This collapsed the formal choice set into Agent_1's preferred baseline and produced unanimous acceptance.
phase_notes.private_voting: Private voting is present for all nine agent-proposal combinations. Agent_1 accepted because the allocation maximized its direct utility, Agent_2 accepted because the same allocation yielded 53, and Agent_3 accepted because it secured Jewel and kept open the possibility of later upgrades.
phase_notes.reflection: No reflection section is present in the transcript.

mechanisms.theme: First speaker anchored the allocation around keeping both top items
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1's first move made Apple+Pencil retention the central baseline, so later discussion treated any alternative as something that had to compensate Agent_1 rather than as an equally live option.

evidence.quote: Skeleton B is very unfavorable for me

mechanisms.explanation: The middle agent also benefited from Skeleton A, so Agent_1's preferred allocation gained a second public advocate and later a private voting rationale based on Agent_2's own payoff.

evidence.quote: Skeleton A is the fastest quick baseline
gives Agent_2 a strong, clear package

mechanisms.theme: Agent_3's better current alternative was deferred
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_3 identified a much better bundle for itself, but both the public and private reasoning treated that option as infeasible because it would take away Agent_1's top items. Agent_3 then accepted Skeleton A now in exchange for possible later discussion.

evidence.quote: lock Skeleton A as the Round 1 baseline

mechanisms.theme: Private thinking normalized preserving Agent_1's top items
mechanisms.explanation: The private planning stage shows the agents internalized preservation of Agent_1's Apple+Pencil as a hard constraint for reaching consensus, which made the final outcome easier to justify despite Agent_3's lower payoff.

evidence.quote: Lock Skeleton A as the Round 1 baseline
Push to lock Skeleton A as the baseline

mechanisms.theme: Formal proposal collapse removed a real choice among outcomes
mechanisms.explanation: By proposal time, every agent submitted the same substantive allocation. Unanimous acceptance therefore ratified Agent_1's preferred baseline rather than comparing it against Agent_3's preferred Skeleton B.

evidence.quote: Baseline aligns with Agent_1's top priorities

mechanisms.theme: Counterweight: Agent_3 did receive its highest single item
mechanisms.explanation: The result was not pure exclusion of the last position. Agent_3 accepted partly because Jewel was valuable to it, but that partial satisfaction made it easier for the first-position-favorable package to pass without giving Agent_3 its stronger Jewel+Quill+Stone bundle.
